message:
refactor: apply Clippy idiom improvements across 16 files

Systematic adoption of more idiomatic Rust patterns flagged by Clippy
lints, improving readability and reducing intermediate closures:

map_or / map_or(0, ...) instead of .map(...).unwrap_or(0):
  - channel/{clock_skew,crash,fault,partition}.rs: emit_*_evidence timestamp
  - evidence_sink.rs: CollectorSink::len, emit_{scheduler,cancel,budget}_evidence
  - runtime/blocking_pool.rs: BlockingPool Debug impl thread count
  - runtime/scheduler/three_lane.rs: queue_depth, timestamp, worker priority
  - transport/router.rs: SymbolDispatcher Debug impl sink count
  - trace/{crashpack,recorder,replay,streaming}.rs: wall clock timestamps
  - lab/config.rs: LabConfig::new_random seed generation

is_ok_and instead of .lock().map(...).unwrap_or(false):
  - runtime/scheduler/three_lane.rs: local_has_runnable and local_ready_has_work

unwrap_or_default instead of unwrap_or(0) for numeric defaults:
  - runtime/scheduler/three_lane.rs: task priority extraction (3 sites)
  - test_ndjson.rs: thread_id_u64 parse fallback

checked_div for safe division:
  - combinator/rate_limit.rs: avg_wait_time calculation avoids potential
    division by zero (though currently guarded, checked_div is clearer)
  - time/wheel.rs: coalescing window computation uses checked_div with
    Option chaining for cleaner overflow handling

Also: channel/watch.rs: explicit drop(waiters) before returning false
to release the lock before the early return path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -92,8 +92,7 @@ impl TraceMetadata {
             seed,
             recorded_at: std::time::SystemTime::now()
                 .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)
-                .map(|d| d.as_nanos() as u64)
-                .unwrap_or(0),
+                .map_or(0, |d| d.as_nanos() as u64),
             config_hash: 0,
             description: None,
         }

@@ -856,10 +856,7 @@ impl std::fmt::Debug for SymbolDispatcher {
             .field("total_failures", &self.total_failures)
             .field(
                 "sinks",
-                &format_args!(
-                    "<{} sinks>",
-                    self.sinks.read().map(|s| s.len()).unwrap_or(0)
-                ),
+                &format_args!("<{} sinks>", self.sinks.read().map_or(0, |s| s.len())),
             )
             .finish()
     }
